sections.paragraph:
( Jud. 20:34 ) fut. יִכְבַּד.

(1) to be heavy (Ethiop. ከበደ፡ id. In Arabic there is but one trace of this signification in كَبَدَ Conj. III. to bear up under any thing, to endure adversity [“and so in Syr. ܐܬܶܟܰܒܰܕ to be indignant, angry”]). Prop. used of weight, Job 6:3. Mostly used figuratively, thus-

(2) to be heavy, to be honoured (Gr. βαρύς, Germ. gewichtig), Job 14:21 Eze 27:25 Isa 66:5. Compare כָּבֹוד.

(3) i.q. to be great, vehement, plentiful, of enormity of wickedness, Gen 18:20 of a battle becoming fierce, Jud. 20:34 1Sa 31:3 of a weight, i.e. a plenty of sand, Job loc. cit.

And in a bad sense-(4) to be troublesome, burdensome, followed by עַל Isa 24:20 Neh 5:18 2Sa 13:25, וְלֹא נִכְבַּד עָלֶיךָ “lest we should be burdensome to thee;” compare 14:26 . כָּֽבְדָה יַד־יְהֹוָה עַל “the hand of God is heavy upon” any one, i.e. God afflicts some one heavily (compare βαρείας χεῖρας Hom.); 1Sa 5:11 Psa 32:4. Followed by אֶל 1Sa 5:6. Job 23:2, יָדִי כָֽבְדָה עַל אַנְחָתִי “the hand of God which presseth on me is heavier than my sighing,” i.e. the calamities which oppress me are more weighty, or more vehement than my complainings; compare יָד letter f. (In Arabic also verbs of weight, such as كبر, ثقل followed by على denote trouble.) From heavy things not being easily moved, this verb is applied-

(5) to indolence, dullness (Schwerfälligkeit), and to any hindrance of the use of the senses; hence to be dull, sluggish, of the eyes, Gen 48:10 of the ears, Isa 59:1 fchwerhörig); also of the mind not easily moved, and therefore obdurate, Exo 9:7 (compare קָשָׁה). In like manner verbs of fatness are applied to indolence; compare חֵלֶב, טָפַשׁ.

Piel כִּבֵּד-

(1) causat. of Kal No. 2, to honour, to do honour to persons, Jud. 13:17 2Sa 10:3 God, Isa 29:13 followed by לְ of person, Psa 86:9 Dan 11:38 and (in the same manner as verbs of abundance) with the two accusatives. Isa 43:23, זְבָהֶיךָ לֹא כִבַּדְתָּנִי “thou hast not honoured me with thy sacrifices.”

(2) cansat. of Kal No. 5, to harden the heart, or mind, 1Sa 6:6.

Pual כֻּבַּד to be honoured, Pro 13:18 27:18 Isa 58:13.

Hiphil-

(1) to make heavy, e.g. a yoke, 1Ki 12:10 Isa 47:6 a chain, Lam 3:7. Ellipt. Neh 5:15, “the former governors הִכְבִּידוּ עַל הָעַם sc. עֹל, laid a heavy (yoke) upon the people,” greatly oppressed the people.

(2) causat. of Kal No. 2, to honour, to increase with honours, to render illustrious, Isa. 8:23 Jer 30:19. Also, to acquire honour or glory (for oneself), 2Ch 25:19.

(3) causat. of Kal No. 5, to make dull the ears, Isa 6:10 Zec 7:11 to harden the heart, Exo 9:34.

Niphal-

(1) pass. of Piel No. 1, to be honoured, to be held in honour, Gen 34:19 1Sa 9:6 2Sa 23:19, 23 2Sa 23:23. שֵׁם נִכְבָּד a glorious name, Deu 28:58. Pl. נִכְבָּדֹות things done gloriously, Psa 87:3.

(2) reflect. to shew oneself great or glorious, Hag 1:8 followed by בְּ in any thing, Exo 14:4 -1718 ; Lev 10:3 Eze 39:13.

(3) to be heavy, i.e. abundant, to be rich, see Kal No. 3. Pro 8:24, מַעְיָנֹות נִכְבַּדֵּי מַיִם “heavy (i.e. copious) fountains of water.” Isa 23:8, Isa 23:9, נִכְבַּדֵּי אֶרֶץ “the most wealthy of the earth.”

Hithpael-

(1) to honour oneself, to boast oneself, Pro 12:9.

(2) to multiply oneself; hence to be numerous, many, Nah 3:15.

The derived nouns follow, except כָּבֹוד.

כבד

const. כְּבַד Exo 4:10, and כֶּבֶד Isa 1:4.

(A) adj.-

(1) heavy, 1Sa 4:18 Pro 27:3 also, laden ( Isa 1:4). Mostly used figuratively, as-

(2) abundant (Germ. eine fchwere Menge; Lat. graves pavonum greges. Varr.); חַיִל כָּבֵד a numerous army, 1Ki 10:2 Isa 36:2 also, rich (وre gravis), Gen 13:2. In a bad sense-

(3) grievous, burdensome (drückend), of a heavy famine, Gen 12:10 41:31 of enormous sin, Psal. 38:5 (compare Isa 1:4, כֶּבֶד עָֹון “(a people) laden with iniquity.”

(4) difficult, hard, of an affair or business, Exo 18:18 Num 11:14 of a language hard to be understood, Eze 3:5.

(5) not easily moved because of weight; slow, of the tongue, Exo 4:10.

(B) subst. the liver (Arab. كَبْدُ, كِبْدُ, كَبِدُ), as being the heaviest of the viscera, both in weight and in importance, Exo 29:13, 22 Exo 29:22 Lev 3:4, 10 . Lam 2:11, נִשְׁפַּךְ לָאָרֶץ כְּבֵדִי “my liver is poured out upon the earth;” hyperb. spoken of the most severe wounding of the liver, i.e. of the mind.
